_doc: Model aliases for bin/sandbox --model <alias> AND judge selection. Each alias entry is [agent, canonical_model_or_null, [extra_args]]. 'default_judge' is the single plan/impl-review backend when --backend is omitted (provider or provider:variant). 'panel' is the default panel-review judge set (entries are 'provider:variant' or a bare provider; an alias name also works). For codex entries only, the variant accepts an optional ':effort' suffix (minimal|low|medium|high|xhigh|max|ultra — per-model validity comes from ~/.codex/models_cache.json supported_reasoning_levels), e.g. 'codex:gpt-5.5:medium' -> `-c model_reasoning_effort=medium` -- without it, Codex falls back to ~/.codex/config.toml's model_reasoning_effort (often 'high', which is why uncapped panel runs get expensive). Override per-project by shadowing with .agent/models.json; override per-run with --models / --backend / --model. Model strings drift between releases — edit values here when an upstream version changes. Schema is stable; only values move. Current Claude family (2026-07-14): fable=claude-fable-5, opus=claude-opus-4-8, sonnet=claude-sonnet-5, haiku=claude-haiku-4-5. These aliases are only a baseline seed for `tasks models check` — it also probes the model your Claude Code is actually set to (~/.claude/settings.json) since claude has no list command, so a newer model you're running is surfaced even if it's not listed here.
